text: We're about to see a super moon. This only happens a few times a year, because to get a super moon, two things need to happen at the same time. First, you need a full moon. The sun always lights up half the moon, and the moon's phase is our perspective of that from Earth. From here on the ground, we see eight distinct phases. It takes about 29.5 days for the moon to go through all of them, so we see about one full moon every month. But second, for a super moon, you need the moon to be in the right location in its orbit, because it only takes the moon about 27 days to orbit the Earth, and the moon's orbit around us isn't a perfect circle. It's more like an egg shape. It's far this from us, here, and closer to us, here. So when a full moon happens at or within 90% of that closest point, it can look up to 30% brighter and 14% larger than the moon at that furthest point. That's a super moon. And you're going to see it on November 5th, December 4th, and January 3rd. And if you believe there should be more optimistic science and tech stories, follow along.
